activities_projects.title: Comparing Fission: Amoeba vs. Leishmania
activities_projects.objective: Understand the role of cell organization in binary fission
materials: Permanent slides of Amoeba and Leishmania
steps: Observe binary fission in Amoeba and note if it occurs in any plane.
Observe Leishmania (the Kala-azar parasite) and its whip-like flagellum.
Identify why Leishmania divides in a definite orientation (longitudinal).
Sketch the difference in the division planes of both organisms.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Recognizes that the structural complexity of an organism dictates the orientation of cell division.
activities_projects.evaluation: Explanation of why Leishmania requires a fixed plane of division compared to Amoeba (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Fragmentation in Spirogyra
activities_projects.objective: Observe reproduction in simple multicellular organisms
steps: Collect green filaments from a pond and place a strand on a slide.
Observe the spiral chloroplasts to confirm it is Spirogyra.
Look for points where the filament has broken into smaller pieces (fragments).
Explain how each fragment grows into a new individual by simple cell division.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Distinguishes between fragmentation in simple organisms and complex regeneration.
activities_projects.evaluation: Accurate drawing of a Spirogyra filament and the process of fragment growth (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Tissue Culture Research Project
activities_projects.objective: Understand the application of vegetative propagation in a lab
steps: Research the process of removing tissue from the growing tip of a plant.
Explain the role of 'Callus' formation in a nutrient medium containing hormones.
Detail how plantlets are transferred to soil.
List two advantages of tissue culture (e.g., disease-free plants, ornamental growth).
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Explains the modern biotechnological method of producing clones from a single parent plant.
activities_projects.evaluation: Flowchart depicting the stages of tissue culture from explant to plantlet (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Artificial Propagation: Grafting and Layering
activities_projects.objective: Explore human-aided methods of plant reproduction
steps: Research the 'Stock' and 'Scion' used in grafting roses.
Simulate or perform layering by bending a jasmine branch into the soil while still attached to the parent.
Explain why these methods are used for plants that have lost the capacity to produce seeds (e.g., banana, orange).
Compare the genetics of the offspring with the parent.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Identifies the economic and agricultural importance of artificial vegetative propagation.
activities_projects.evaluation: Comparison of offspring traits in artificial propagation vs. sexual reproduction (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The DNA Copying Process
activities_projects.objective: Analyze the importance of variation in reproduction
steps: Read about the biochemical process of DNA replication before cell division.
Explain why no biochemical reaction is 100% reliable.
Describe how slight 'inaccuracies' in DNA copying lead to variations.
Discuss a scenario where variation helps a population of bacteria survive a heatwave.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Relates DNA copying to the inheritance of body design and the survival of species through variation.
activities_projects.evaluation: Paragraph explaining why variation is useful for the species but not necessarily for the individual (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Pollination Mechanisms
steps: Examine flowers for features that attract insects (color, nectar) or catch wind (feathery stigma).
Observe the transfer of pollen from the anther to the stigma of the same flower (self-pollination).
Research how agents like bees, wind, and water facilitate cross-pollination.
Identify the role of the pollen tube in reaching the ovule.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Visualizes the physical transfer of male gametes to female gametes in flowering plants.
activities_projects.evaluation: Table listing three agents of pollination and the specific floral adaptations for each (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Post-Fertilization Changes in Plants
activities_projects.objective: Trace the transformation of a flower into a fruit
steps: Examine a pea pod and identify what part of the flower it was (ovary).
Identify the seeds inside and what they were (ovules).
Observe the dried sepals or style still attached to the fruit.
Write the sequence: Zygote $ightarrow$ Embryo $ightarrow$ Seed.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands the developmental stages of a plant following successful fertilization.
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct identification of the floral origin of the fruit wall and the seed (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The Role of Placenta
activities_projects.objective: Investigate the nutrition and waste exchange in the womb
steps: Identify the placenta as a disc embedded in the uterine wall.
Locate the villi on the embryo's side and blood spaces on the mother's side.
List three substances that move from the mother to the embryo (e.g., glucose, oxygen).
Name the substance that moves from the embryo to the mother (waste/CO2).
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Describes the specialized tissue that facilitates physiological exchange between mother and fetus.
activities_projects.evaluation: Technical description of the surface area adaptation of the placenta (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Menstrual Cycle Basics
activities_projects.objective: Understand the monthly preparation of the female body for pregnancy
steps: Identify why the uterus thickens its lining every month.
Explain what happens if the egg is not fertilized (breakdown of lining).
Note the typical duration of the menstrual cycle (28 days) and the flow (2-8 days).
Discuss the importance of hygiene during this period.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Recognizes menstruation as a natural biological process related to reproductive readiness.
activities_projects.evaluation: Explanation of the hormonal 'trigger' for the shedding of the uterine lining (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Reproductive Health and Contraception
activities_projects.objective: Analyze methods to prevent pregnancy and STDs
materials: Research data on barrier, chemical, and surgical methods
steps: Categorize methods into: Barrier (Condoms), Chemical (Oral pills), and IUCDs (Copper-T).
Identify which methods also protect against STIs like HIV-AIDS.
Research permanent surgical methods: Vasectomy (males) and Tubectomy (females).
Discuss the social impact of the female-to-male sex ratio.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Evaluates different birth control strategies based on their effectiveness and health benefits.
activities_projects.evaluation: Classification of at least four contraceptive methods and their mode of action (10 marks)
